data.title: Hong Kong Urged to Review Regulatory Mechanisms Amid Trust Fraud Concerns
data.description:
According to Odaily, a recent $500 million fraud case involving a trust institution has raised questions about Hong Kong's regulatory framework. Wu Jiezhuang, Chairman of the Legislative Council's Subcommittee on Web3 and Virtual Asset Development, emphasized that while Hong Kong is actively attracting foreign investment, including from mainland China, there is a significant misunderstanding of its systems among outsiders. This issue needs to be addressed promptly.

Wu noted that the incident is likely to draw international attention, necessitating a swift review of the relevant regulatory mechanisms to address public concerns. Currently, Hong Kong lacks a dedicated asset custody regulatory system, and some Web3 companies use trust companies as third-party asset custodians. While following proper procedures poses no issues, exploitation of regulatory gaps by criminals could undermine confidence in Hong Kong as a financial hub.

He suggested increasing efforts in public education and reviewing the system for potential improvements to prevent similar incidents in the future.

data.title: South African Tax Authority Mandates Registration for Crypto Transactions
data.description: According to Odaily, South African Revenue Service Commissioner Edward Kieswetter has announced that all taxpayers, exchanges, and intermediaries involved in cryptocurrency transactions must register with the tax authorities. Failure to do so will be considered illegal. Kieswetter emphasized that registration will enable the tax department to monitor business activities and enhance regulatory oversight of the crypto industry.

data.title: Hong Kong legislators respond to the US$500 million fraud case involving a trust institution: the outside world may have misunderstandings about the Hong Kong system
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News In response to a trust institution being involved in a $500 million fraud case and raising questions about the Hong Kong system, Wu Jiezhuang, chairman of the Hong Kong Legislative Council's Web3 and Virtual Asset Development Subcommittee, said that Hong Kong is actively attracting foreign investment, including from the mainland, but many outsiders have limited understanding of Hong Kong's system and even misunderstandings, which is a problem that needs to be faced.
Wu Jiezhuang pointed out that the incident is expected to attract international attention and the relevant regulatory mechanism must be reviewed as soon as possible to respond to social concerns. At present, Hong Kong has not yet established a special asset custody regulatory system. Some Web3 companies will use trust companies as a means of third-party custody of assets. If they follow the formal process, there is no problem, but if the system is used by criminals, it may affect the outside world's confidence in Hong Kong's financial center.
He suggested that publicity and education should be strengthened, and whether the system has room for optimization should be reviewed to prevent similar incidents from happening again.

data.title: The White House is close to approving the TikTok US transaction. A16z and Blackstone plan to invest and control nearly half of the shares
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the Financial Times on April 3, the White House is close to approving a transaction plan to separate TikTok's US business from ByteDance. Sources revealed that a group of new external investors including a16z, Blackstone Group, Silver Lake Capital, etc. are expected to jointly hold about 50% of TikTok's US business. Existing investors General Atlantic, Susquehanna, KKR and Coatue will continue to hold about 30% of the shares.
According to the terms of the transaction, TikTok's parent company ByteDance will retain less than 20% of the shares to meet the relevant requirements in US legislation that "foreign adversaries" cannot hold controlling stakes. The transaction is intended to meet the compliance requirements of the US law for non-Chinese holdings of TikTok to avoid it from facing a ban in the United States after April 5.
It is reported that the transaction is still in its early stages and due diligence, structural adjustments and financing arrangements will be carried out in the coming months. White House officials plan to hold a meeting on Wednesday to discuss the matter. If President Trump approves, the transaction is expected to be announced as soon as possible. However, sources also reminded that the current situation is still unstable and the White House may still adjust its position.

data.title: South African Revenue Service: Cryptocurrency asset traders must register with the tax center, otherwise it is illegal
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Edward Kieswetter, director of the South African Revenue Service, said that all taxpayers, exchanges and intermediaries involved in crypto asset transactions must complete registration with the tax authorities, otherwise it will be considered an illegal act. Kieswetter said that after completing the registration, the tax department will be able to track related business activities and strengthen supervision of the crypto industry. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Justin Sun: FDT is insolvent and suspected of fraud, calling on Hong Kong regulators to intervene in the investigation
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Sun Yuchen wrote that First Digital Trust (FDT) has fallen into a state of insolvency and is unable to fulfill its obligations to repay customer funds. Despite major violations, the institution continues to operate in Hong Kong under the name of a public trust. He said that this exposes loopholes in the supervision of Hong Kong's trust industry, which not only threatens the public interest, but also damages the reputation of Hong Kong as a financial center.
Sun Yuchen expressed support for Techteryx, which has initiated civil lawsuits and criminal reports in many places including the Hong Kong High Court and the U.S. Department of Justice, and hired top global law firms to promote accountability. He called on Hong Kong regulatory and law enforcement agencies to take action as soon as possible to prevent such behavior from continuing to occur under the name of licensed institutions.

data.title: Fed Governor: Tariff impact may be more lasting, supports keeping policy unchanged
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Fed Governor Adriana Kugler said early Thursday that it is appropriate to maintain the current interest rate level until the upward risks of inflation subside. In her prepared speech, she pointed out that changes in government policies, the recent stagnation of the cooling process of inflation, and rising inflation expectations are all key reasons for maintaining policy patience. 
"As long as the upward risks of inflation persist and economic activity and employment remain stable, I will support maintaining the current policy interest rate." Although in theory the impact of one-time tariffs on inflation should be short-lived, if it affects multiple areas of the economy and further pushes up inflation expectations, its impact may be more lasting. 
Data show that since reaching a 40-year high in 2022, the inflation level has improved limitedly recently. A survey by the University of Michigan showed that consumers' long-term inflation expectations climbed to a 32-year high in March. Kugler focused on the importance of stabilizing inflation expectations, pointing out that both short-term and long-term inflation expectations have risen recently. (Jinshi)

data.title: Curve Finance Achieves Record Trading Volume in Early 2025
data.description: According to PANews, decentralized lending protocol and exchange Curve Finance reported a record trading volume of nearly $35 billion in the first quarter of 2025. This marks an increase of over 13% compared to the first quarter of 2024. The surge in trading volume is attributed to a significant rise in the number of transactions, which grew from approximately 1.8 million in the first quarter of 2024 to about 5.5 million in the same period of 2025. This robust growth occurred despite an overall decline in the cryptocurrency market. Data from CoinGecko indicates that the total market capitalization of cryptocurrencies has fallen by more than 20% since the beginning of the year, as of March 31.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Experience Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the yields on U.S. 10-year and 2-year Treasury futures fell by 10 basis points, reaching 4.06% and 3.798%, respectively.

data.title: The yields on U.S. 10-year and 2-year Treasury futures fell 10 basis points to 4.06% and 3.798% respectively.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: On April 3, the yields of the US 10-year and 2-year Treasury futures fell by 10 basis points to 4.06% and 3.798% respectively. (Jinshi)
